title: conostylis setigera subsp. dasys
intro: Conostylis setigera subsp. dasys is a subspecies of the Conostylis setigera plant, commonly known as the 'hairy finger' or 'hairy blue-rush', which is a type of sedge found in the southwestern regions of Western Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing, reducing inflammation, and fighting off infections. The health benefits of Conostylis setigera subsp. dasys include the treatment of skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as the relief of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Conostylis setigera subsp. dasys can be prepared into herbal remedies like teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
